 {"id":"bd-tnu","title":"Docs: Add AGENTS.md playbooks (cass rituals + alien artifact + optimization)","description":"Goal: make agent workflow more repeatable by adding short, high-signal playbooks to AGENTS.md.

Deliverables:
- Session-start ritual (cass-mined prompts) + command checklist.
- Cass archaeology workflow (status/index/search/view/expand/context).
- Alien-artifact elicitation prompt + evidence-ledger / loss-matrix reminder.
- Extreme optimization protocol: baseline/profile/isomorphism proof + golden checksums.
- Porting-to-rust essence extraction checklist (spec-first, conformance-first).

Acceptance:
- AGENTS.md gains a new Playbooks section with copy-paste-ready snippets.
- No contradictory guidance vs existing rules (no file deletion, no destructive commands, use cargo, run gates).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:26:00.865935325Z","created_by":"QuietFalcon","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:27:13.879183709Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:27:13.879154274Z","close_reason":"Add playbooks to AGENTS.md (cass workflow, alien artifact prompt, optimization loop, porting checklist)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}

 {"id":"bd-u2g7m","title":"[FUZZ-TARGET] add ffs-core mounted-image byte-corruption recovery harness","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to the `ffs-core` open/mount recovery boundary with raw byte corruption as the driver. Build a libFuzzer harness that mutates minimally valid ext4 and btrfs images, then opens them through `OpenFs::from_device(...)` / `OpenFs::open_with_options(...)` while recovery-related knobs vary. The harness should focus on repair/recover paths instead of only parser entry points.

Primary targets:
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2132-2387` mount/open path
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2755-2929` ext4 crash/orphan recovery
- `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2204-2235` btrfs tree-log recovery branch

Coverage goals:
- random byte corruption in superblocks, root-tree blocks, orphan links, log roots, and recovery flags
- deterministic classification into `FfsError`, safe replay skip, or successful mount
- no panic, no hang, no unbounded traversal during corrupted-image recovery

Acceptance:
- new fuzz target added under `fuzz/fuzz_targets/` and registered in `fuzz/Cargo.toml`
- shared corpus seeded from current clean/dirty ext4 and btrfs recovery fixtures
- crashers minimized and promoted to regressions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:34:30Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:18:00Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:18:00Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["FUZZ-TARGET","ffs-core","fuzzing","mount-path","recovery","repair","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-u355g","title":"[HIGH] open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree smoke assertions are stale against default-root mount semantics","description":"# Review finding

The `build_btrfs_image()` smoke suite is failing early today on missing default-root resolution, but the test body behind `open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree` is also stale against the current mount contract. It still asserts that the walked root tree contains exactly one item with `objectid=256` and an 8-byte dummy payload.

## Evidence
- Failing suite today: `tests::open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree` is in the red set from `cargo test --workspace --all-targets`, but it currently aborts before its assertions because `OpenFs::from_device(..., OpenOptions::default())` cannot resolve a default-root `ROOT_ITEM`.
- Test assertions: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:22736-22748` expect `items.len() == 1`, `items[0].key.objectid == 256`, `item_type == 132`, and dummy payload bytes.
- Current selection logic opens btrfs under `BtrfsMountSelection::DefaultRoot` when no selector is supplied: `crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:2395-2428`. Any fixture fix that adds the missing default-root `ROOT_ITEM` for objectid 5 or otherwise models current semantics will likely invalidate the old single-item/objectid-256 expectation unless the test explicitly opens a non-default selection.

## Why this is high severity
This is a likely second workspace-red failure masked by the earlier `NotFound` abort. If the fixture is repaired without aligning this test, the gate will still stay red.

## Repro
Current gate: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets`

## Expected fix direction
Either:
- make the smoke test open an explicit non-default/root-tree-focused selection and keep the current objectid-256 assertions, or
- update the fixture and assertions to reflect the default-root mount contract and the resulting walked root-tree inventory.
Do not stop at making `OpenFs::from_device()` succeed; prove the post-open root-tree assertions still match the intended contract.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:34:34.746033272Z","created_by":"cod-fs","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:28Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:28Z","close_reason":"Completed: open_fs_btrfs_walk_root_tree now asserts the parseable default-root contract and passes in the targeted btrfs smoke suite","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ffs-core","qa","review","tests","workspace-gate"]}
 {"id":"bd-u38n","title":"Add proptest property-based tests to ffs-xattr","description":"Add property-based tests for: (1) encode_entries_region/parse roundtrip - encoded entries survive decode regardless of insertion order, (2) set_xattr/get_xattr roundtrip with arbitrary names and values","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:19:28.130537158Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:31:25.088882691Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:31:25.088860219Z","close_reason":"Added 6 proptest property-based tests to ffs-xattr: set/get roundtrip, set/remove/get-none, overwrite preserves others, set-multiple list complete, encode/parse external roundtrip, remove-all-leaves-empty. All gates clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
